text.title: AI-Agent Gotchas and Best Practices
text.summary: Critical failure modes when AI agents interact with security tooling, and best practices for secure-by-default pipeline operation.

antipattern.description: Agent widens allowlists or suppresses SAST rules to make findings go away
antipattern.reason: Suppression hides real vulnerabilities. Lock .semgrepignore and .snyk behind a separate review path; require a one-line rationale and review-by date on every suppression.

antipattern.description: Agent writes LLM-generated regex for input validation
antipattern.reason: LLM-generated regex is famously bypassable. Use battle-tested libraries (bleach, defusedxml, validators, email-validator) and pin versions.

antipattern.description: Agent auto-merges Dependabot/Snyk PRs without integration tests
antipattern.reason: Dependency updates break prod weekly without a green build + integration test gate on the changed dependency.

antipattern.description: Agent hallucinates CVE IDs and remediation steps from training data
antipattern.reason: Always cross-check findings against NVD (nvd.nist.gov) or osv.dev before acting.

antipattern.description: Agent generates crypto primitives ("here's a custom AES-GCM wrapper")
antipattern.reason: Custom crypto is the most dangerous code class. Force cryptography.fernet, libsodium, or KMS-managed keys. Reject ad-hoc primitives.

antipattern.description: Agent runs DAST automation against production URLs
antipattern.reason: DAST scripts that mutate prod data cause real side effects. Restrict to non-prod URLs in CI policy and by ACL.

rule.statement: Define a triage process before connecting any scanner to CI. Pre-existing findings get one triage decision; only new findings block PRs.
rule.rationale: High false-positive rates from DAST and SCA erode trust in the pipeline. A triage process prevents "turn it off" reactions.

rule.statement: Sign artifacts (cosign/sigstore), pin base images by digest, generate SBOM (syft), and scan the SBOM (grype) as part of every container build.
rule.rationale: Supply chain attacks (OWASP A03 2025) target build artifacts and base images. Signing + SBOM provides provenance verification.

rule.statement: Human-in-loop on every Critical finding. Agents may auto-open PRs for Low/Medium findings with regression tests; Critical findings require human review before merge.
rule.rationale: The business and legal consequences of Critical vulnerabilities require human judgment about risk acceptance, timing, and disclosure.

rule.statement: Run penetration testing annually and after major architectural changes. Automated scanners do not replace pentesters for logic flaws and novel attack chains.
rule.rationale: Scanners find what they were programmed to find. Pentesters find what attackers find — including combinations of low-severity findings that chain into critical exploits.
